annotations.label: Vector bundle

anchor.snippet: a vector bundle is a topological construction that makes precise the idea of a family of vector spaces parameterized by another space

annotations.note: `VectorBundle R F E` is the class of a fiberwise-linear locally trivial bundle, the exact notion.

annotations.label: Trivial bundle (constant family)

anchor.snippet: Such vector bundles are said to be trivial

annotations.note: `Bundle.Trivial B F` with its `vectorBundle` instance is the constant/trivial vector bundle.

annotations.label: Tangent bundle of a manifold

anchor.snippet: class of examples are the tangent bundles of smooth (or differentiable) manifolds

annotations.note: `tangentBundleCore I M` builds the tangent bundle `TangentBundle I M` as a smooth vector bundle.

annotations.label: Parallelizable manifold

anchor.snippet: a manifold is said to be parallelizable if, and only if, its tangent bundle is trivial

annotations.note: No definition of parallelizability (trivial tangent bundle) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Local triviality (fiber bundle)

anchor.snippet: Vector bundles are almost always required to be locally trivial

annotations.note: The `FiberBundle` class encodes local triviality via a trivialization atlas with a trivialization at each base point.

annotations.label: Real vector bundle

anchor.snippet: A real vector bundle consists of

annotations.note: A real vector bundle is `VectorBundle ℝ F E`, the instantiation of the general field-parametrized class at `ℝ`.

annotations.label: Local trivialization

anchor.snippet: is called a local trivialization of the vector bundle

annotations.note: `Bundle.Trivialization F (π F E)` is exactly a local trivialization (with `IsLinear` for the vector-bundle case).

annotations.label: Rank of a vector bundle

anchor.snippet: is called the rank of the vector bundle

annotations.note: There is no named `rank`, but `VectorBundle.finrank_eq` proves every fiber has the constant rank `finrank R F`.

annotations.label: Line bundle and plane bundle

anchor.snippet: Vector bundles of rank 1 are called line bundles

annotations.note: No dedicated line-bundle or plane-bundle definition (rank-1/rank-2 bundles) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trivial bundle of rank k

anchor.snippet: is called the trivial bundle of rank

annotations.note: `Bundle.Trivial B F` generalizes the rank-`k` trivial bundle (take `F = Fin k → R`).

annotations.label: Transition functions

anchor.snippet: These are called the transition functions

annotations.note: Transition functions appear as `coordChange` fields of `FiberBundleCore`/`VectorBundleCore` and as `Trivialization.coordChangeL`.

annotations.label: Transition functions form a Čech cocycle

anchor.snippet: The set of transition functions forms a Čech cocycle in the sense that

annotations.note: `FiberBundleCore.coordChange_comp` is exactly the cocycle identity `coordChange j k ∘ coordChange i j = coordChange i k`.

annotations.label: Fibre bundle construction theorem

anchor.snippet: Conversely, given a fiber bundle

annotations.note: `FiberBundleCore` (with `VectorBundleCore.toVectorBundle`) reconstructs a bundle from cocycle transition data, the construction theorem.

annotations.label: Subbundle

anchor.snippet: a subbundle is simply a topological subspace

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a subbundle of a vector bundle.

annotations.label: Bundles as subbundles of trivial bundles

anchor.snippet: A subbundle of a trivial bundle need not be trivial

annotations.note: No subbundle theory exists, so this statement about subbundles of trivial bundles is absent.

annotations.label: Vector bundle morphism

anchor.snippet: A morphism from the vector bundle

annotations.note: There is no structure for a morphism between two vector bundles in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: A morphism from the vector bundle

annotations.label: Category of vector bundles

anchor.snippet: The class of all vector bundles together with bundle morphisms forms a category

annotations.note: No `CategoryTheory` instance for vector bundles is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bundle isomorphism and trivialization

anchor.snippet: is called a (vector) bundle isomorphism

annotations.note: Trivializations (fiberwise-linear isos to the trivial bundle) are formalized, but a general bundle isomorphism between two arbitrary bundles is not.

annotations.label: Category over a fixed base space

anchor.snippet: We can also consider the category of all vector bundles over a fixed base space

annotations.note: No category of vector bundles over a fixed base is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Morphism as morphism into pullback bundle

anchor.snippet: can also be viewed as a vector bundle morphism over

annotations.note: Without a bundle-morphism notion this equivalence (covering map vs. pullback morphism) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Section of a vector bundle

anchor.snippet: we can consider sections of π on U

annotations.note: `ContMDiffSection` formalizes smooth global sections; general continuous sections over an open set are not a dedicated type.

annotations.label: Zero section and sheaf of sections

anchor.snippet: always contains at least one element, namely the zero section

annotations.note: `Bundle.zeroSection` is formalized, but the sheaf of sections of a vector bundle is not constructed.

annotations.label: Sections form an O_X-module

anchor.snippet: is a module over the ring of continuous real-valued functions on

annotations.note: Smooth sections form a module over the field and `smul_section` shows scaling by functions stays smooth, but no explicit module structure over the function ring is built.

annotations.label: Vector bundles give locally free sheaves

anchor.snippet: arises in this fashion from a vector bundle: only the locally free ones do

annotations.note: No link between topological vector bundles and locally free sheaves of their sections is formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalence with locally free sheaves

anchor.snippet: the category of real vector bundles on X is equivalent to the category of locally free and finitely generated sheaves

annotations.note: This category equivalence between real vector bundles and locally free f.g. sheaves is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Triviality via global sections

anchor.snippet: A rank n vector bundle is trivial if and only if it has n linearly independent global sections

annotations.note: Local frames (`IsLocalFrameOn`) are formalized as related infrastructure, but the trivial-iff-global-frame criterion is not proved.

annotations.label: Dual bundle

anchor.snippet: called the dual bundle

annotations.note: No dual vector bundle construction exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Whitney sum (direct sum bundle)

anchor.snippet: The Whitney sum (named for Hassler Whitney ) or direct sum bundle

annotations.note: `VectorBundle.prod` gives the fiberwise direct sum `E₁ ×ᵇ E₂`, i.e. the Whitney sum.

annotations.label: Tensor product bundle

anchor.snippet: is defined in a similar way, using fiberwise tensor product of vector spaces

annotations.note: No fiberwise tensor-product bundle construction exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hom-bundle

anchor.snippet: is a vector bundle whose fiber at x is the space of linear maps

annotations.note: `Bundle.ContinuousLinearMap.vectorBundle` is the bundle whose fiber is `E₁ x →SL[σ] E₂ x`, the Hom-bundle.

annotations.label: Eigenbundle construction

anchor.snippet: one can construct an eigenbundle

annotations.note: No eigenbundle construction (fiberwise eigenspaces of a bundle endomorphism) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Canonical isomorphism Hom(E,F)=E*⊗F

anchor.snippet: There is a canonical vector bundle isomorphism

annotations.note: Neither the dual nor the tensor-product bundle exists, so this canonical isomorphism is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pullback bundle

anchor.snippet: An operation of a different nature is the pullback bundle construction

annotations.note: `VectorBundle.pullback` gives the vector-bundle structure on the pullback `f *ᵖ E`.

annotations.label: Bundles over compact space are direct summands

anchor.snippet: Any vector bundle E over X is a direct summand of a trivial bundle

annotations.note: The theorem that a bundle over a compact base embeds as a summand of a trivial bundle is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector bundle metric

anchor.snippet: vector bundles may be equipped with a vector bundle metric

annotations.note: `RiemannianBundle`/`IsContinuousRiemannianBundle` equip a real vector bundle with a continuous fiberwise inner product, i.e. a bundle metric.

annotations.label: Complex vector bundle

anchor.snippet: A vector bundle with a complex structure corresponds to a complex vector bundle

annotations.note: A complex vector bundle is `VectorBundle ℂ F E`; the correspondence with real bundles carrying a complex structure is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Banach bundle

anchor.snippet: the fiber F is taken to be a Banach space then a Banach bundle is obtained

annotations.note: `VectorBundle` already allows the model fiber to be any normed space, subsuming Banach fibers, but no named Banach-bundle notion exists.

annotations.label: Vector bundles as special fiber bundles

anchor.snippet: Vector bundles are special fiber bundles

annotations.note: `VectorBundle R F E` is defined as a mixin requiring `[FiberBundle F E]`, so every vector bundle is by design a fiber bundle.

annotations.label: Smooth vector bundle

anchor.snippet: is smooth , if E and M are smooth manifolds

annotations.note: `ContMDiffVectorBundle n F E IB` is the class of `C^n` (in particular smooth) vector bundles.

annotations.label: Characterization by smooth transition functions

anchor.snippet: A smooth vector bundle can be characterized by the fact that it admits transition functions

annotations.note: `ContMDiffVectorBundle` is defined precisely as having `C^n` transition functions (the `contMDiffOn_coordChange` condition).

annotations.label: C^r, analytic, holomorphic, algebraic bundles

anchor.snippet: then the vector bundle is a C r vector bundle

annotations.note: `ContMDiffVectorBundle` with smoothness index in `ℕ∞ω` covers `C^r` and analytic (`ω`) bundles, but holomorphic and algebraic bundles are not formalized.

annotations.label: Vertical lift identification of tangent space

anchor.snippet: can be naturally identified with the fibre E x itself

annotations.note: The identification of the vertical tangent space of `E` with the fiber `Eₓ` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Canonical vector field

anchor.snippet: known as the canonical vector field

annotations.note: The canonical (Euler/Liouville) vector field on a vector bundle is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Canonical vector field characterizes bundle structure

anchor.snippet: characterizes completely the smooth vector bundle structure in the following manner

annotations.note: No theorem characterizing the vector-bundle structure via the canonical vector field exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Secondary vector bundle structure on TE

anchor.snippet: has a natural secondary vector bundle structure

annotations.note: The secondary vector-bundle structure on the tangent bundle `TE` is not formalized.

annotations.label: K-theory group

anchor.snippet: is defined as the abelian group generated by isomorphism classes

annotations.note: Topological K-theory (the Grothendieck group of vector bundles) is not in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: is defined as the abelian group generated by isomorphism classes

annotations.label: Bott periodicity theorem

anchor.snippet: The famous periodicity theorem of Raoul Bott asserts that the K-theory of any space

annotations.note: Bott periodicity is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: K-theory of sheaves and bundles on smooth schemes

anchor.snippet: one considers the K-theory groups consisting of coherent sheaves on a scheme

annotations.note: Algebraic K-theory of coherent sheaves/bundles on schemes is not in Mathlib.
